terminology: BVR/RWS: Beyond Visual Range/Range While Search

source.section: Part 10 - Offence: Weapons & Armament > 3 - Air-to-Air Weapons > 3.4 - AIM-7M Sparrow (Radar)

steps.action: Set RDR ATTK page on the right DDI and the SMS page on the left DDI (Digital Display Indicator) by pressing the MENU OSB, then selecting TAC or SUPT page, then choosing which menu to display on which DDI.
Weapon Select Switch – SPARROW, or « LSHIFT+W »
Master Mode switch will be automatically set to A/A
On SMS (Stores Management System) page, set your desired target size (SMALL/MEDIUM/LARGE).

Press OSB next to MODE to select missile mode. NORM is a normal launch, LOFT is to make the missile fly a higher trajectory to face less dense air and thus increase energy during hitting the target, and HELO is specifically use for helicopters. In our case, we will use NORM.
If you have different types of Sparrows loaded, select desired Sparrow type on the SMS page.

When SPARROW is selected, radar is operating, press the Sensor Select Switch RIGHT select BVR/RWS (Beyond Visual Range/Range While Search) radar mode and slave the TDC (Throttle Designation Controller) to the radar screen.
Set desired radar range scale (40 nm in our case)
Set desired radar azimuth range (140 deg in our case)

Set desired radar bar mode (4 or 2 bars are generally used) Undesignate / Nosewheel Steering Button Sensor Control Switch AFT/FWD/LEFT/RIGHT TDC is active on Right DDI RWS (Range While Search) Radar Contact Radar Contact Radar Contact Weapon Select Switch FWD: Select Sparrow AFT: Select Gun PRESS DOWN: Select Sidewinder RIGHT: Select Amraam LEFT: No Function
Control your antenna elevation to make sure the radar scans the desired area.
Move the TDC over the target using the TDC controls.
Lock target by using the TDC DEPRESS control (« ENTER »).
Fly to place target in ASE circle on the HUD to lock it on radar. When target is flying through this scan zone, it is automatically locked on to in STT (Single Target Track) mode

Fly to place the Steering Dot inside the ASE/NIRD (Allowable Steering Error / Normalized In-Range Display) Circle and squeeze the gun trigger (« Spacebar ») when you see the SHOOT cue over the TD (Target Designation) box on the HUD to launch missile. Note 1: You can unlock a target by pressing the Undesignate Button Note 2: You can also use ACM (Air Combat Maneuvering) radar modes to lock a target, as shown in the AIM-9 Sidewinder tutorial.
